questions.question: The diathesis-stress model proposes that psychological disorders result from:

options.text: Exclusively genetic factors passed down through families
The interaction between a predisposition toward a disorder and environmental stressors
Poor coping skills that develop during adolescence
Supernatural forces that cannot be measured scientifically

questions.explanation: The diathesis-stress model proposes that psychological disorders develop when a predisposition (diathesis) -- which may be biological, psychological, or social -- interacts with environmental stressors. Neither the predisposition nor the stress alone is sufficient.

questions.question: Which model considers biological, psychological, and social factors together when explaining psychological disorders?
questions.explanation: The biopsychosocial model is an integrative approach that considers how biological factors (genetics, neurochemistry), psychological factors (cognition, coping), and social factors (culture, relationships) interact to produce and maintain psychological disorders.
